w:r.w:t: This document covers three high-impact features to be added to the QUEST platform: a dynamic Points System, Adaptive Difficulty Scaling, and a Round Swap System. Each feature is fully specced with formulas, build checklists, and edge case handling.
Replaces the current rounds-completed + total-time leaderboard with a live points-based ranking visible to all players and spectators in real time.
Every completed round awards points across three components:
Round points = Base points + Difficulty bonus + Time tier bonus
Every completed round awards 200 points regardless of speed or difficulty. This guarantees even slow teams always earn something for finishing.
Rounds tagged as Hard award an additional 283 points on top of base. This value is the average of the three time tier bonuses: (500 + 250 + 100) / 3 = 283.
Time is measured from the moment the Solver receives the puzzle to the moment the Runner completes the mini-game (full round clock).
Maximum (all Hard rounds, all under 5 min)
Minimum (all Normal rounds, all over 15 min)
Points update live the moment a round is cleared — no refresh needed
Leaderboard visible to all players, spectators, and admins throughout the event
Teams can view their own per-round breakdown in-app: base + difficulty bonus + time bonus
Leaderboard sorted by total points descending; total time used as tiebreaker
Add difficulty tier field (Normal / Hard) to round schema
Implement round clock: start on Solver puzzle delivery, end on Runner mini-game completion
Calculate time tier on round clear and persist to DB
Calculate and store per-round score breakdown (base + difficulty + time)
Expose team score breakdown endpoint for in-app display
Update leaderboard to display points, sorted descending
Leaderboard real-time sync on round clear via existing Socket.IO pipeline

Fast-moving teams are automatically assigned harder mini-game parameters and higher-tier puzzles as rounds progress. Slower teams maintain standard parameters. Difficulty escalation also increases the points a team can earn.
After each round, calculate the team's average round completion time
Compare against a pre-set par time (configured by admin before the event)
If a team's average is consistently faster than par, escalate next round's difficulty
If a team is at or slower than par, maintain current difficulty tier
Cooldown rule: difficulty cannot escalate two consecutive rounds without a stabilisation check
Puzzles are pre-tagged with difficulty tiers (Normal / Hard) before the event
System assigns next puzzle tier based on team's current difficulty level
Hard puzzles award the 283 pt difficulty bonus on the points system

Adaptive difficulty and the points system are directly linked. Teams escalated to Hard difficulty earn the +283 difficulty bonus on top of their time tier bonus. Faster teams earn more points per round but face harder challenges — creating a natural self-balancing competition.
Admin panel shows current difficulty tier per team in real time
Admin can manually override a team's difficulty tier for the next round
Par time is configured per round during event setup
Maximum difficulty capped so challenges remain completable — no infinite escalation
Teams that were slow due to a help request or technical issue are excluded from that round's average when calculating escalation
Cooldown: no escalation two rounds in a row without a stabilisation check (team must sustain pace)
Add par time field to round schema, configurable per round in admin panel
Calculate team average completion time after each round
Implement escalation logic: compare average vs par, apply cooldown rule
Pass difficulty tier to mini-game initialisation for each round
Update Target Lock: honour time limit and tap count from tier config
Update Neural Decode: honour grid size and flip-back delay from tier config
Update Cipher Crack: honour sequence length and flash duration from tier config
Tag solver puzzles with difficulty tier in DB
Assign correct puzzle tier based on team's current escalation state
Expose team difficulty tier on admin panel with manual override
Connect difficulty tier to points system difficulty bonus

When a round location becomes physically inaccessible or unusable, admin can swap the affected round with a substitute from a pre-loaded reserve pool. The swap applies to all teams, with each team's current progress determining how the swap is applied.
Admin pre-loads 5 to 8 spare puzzles into a reserve pool before the event starts
Each spare puzzle tagged with difficulty tier to match the round it may replace
Spare QR code locations pre-planted in the field by volunteers before the event
Once a spare is used for a swap, the same substitute location and puzzle apply to all teams
Round location becomes physically inaccessible (locked building, safety issue)
GPS presence verification is failing for all teams at a location (signal dead zone)
Admin decides to remove a round from the event for any operational reason
Note: damaged or stolen QR codes are handled operationally by on-site volunteers who carry replacement QR codes. A swap is not required for this scenario.
When a swap is triggered, each team falls into one of three states:
Their next upcoming round is replaced by the substitute
No change — they finish the current round as normal
Rerouted to substitute location; Solver receives substitute puzzle
The passkey entry event is the cutoff point. Since passkey entry is a verified server-side event, the system can determine each team's bucket precisely and apply the correct action automatically.
Admin selects the round to swap from the admin panel
System displays the reserve pool; admin selects the substitute puzzle and confirms the substitute location
System evaluates each team's state and applies the correct action per the table above
Runner receives rerouting notification instantly if they have not yet scanned
Solver receives substitute puzzle instantly if team has not yet scanned
Teams that have already scanned are unaffected and complete the original round normally
Substitute rounds count normally for points and time — no penalty or advantage
Admin-swapped rounds flagged with an indicator on the post-event results export
Option to include or exclude flagged rounds from the final time calculation in post-event review
If a team is mid-mini-game when the swap triggers, they are not interrupted — they complete the mini-game and the round counts normally
Swap confirmation requires admin to confirm the action — no accidental triggers
A swap only affects the selected round — the next round unlocks normally for all teams after the substitute is cleared
If all reserve pool puzzles are exhausted, admin is warned and must resolve manually
Reserve pool schema: store spare puzzles with difficulty tier, location coordinates, QR reference
Admin panel: reserve pool view with available spares listed
Admin panel: swap trigger UI — select round, select spare, confirm
Backend: evaluate each team state on swap trigger (completed / passkey-entered / not-yet-scanned)
Backend: apply correct action per team state (no-change / next-round-replace / immediate-reroute)
Runner interface: rerouting notification and map update on swap
Solver interface: substitute puzzle delivery on swap
Leaderboard: flag swapped rounds in post-event export
Guard: warn admin when reserve pool is running low (1 spare remaining)
Guard: prevent swap trigger without confirmation step
Par time config, mini-game tier params, points system
Reserve pool schema, team state evaluation logic
QUEST: The Code Scavenger — Built for the 2026 College Tech Fest
